Policies › Information Security Policy

Information Security Policy

NexusPoint Systems Information Security Policy

Document Version: 2.3
Effective Date: 1 March 2024
Next Review: 1 March 2025
Owner: Marcus Webb, Cybersecurity Lead
Approved By: Alex Nguyen, Managing Director

1. Purpose and Scope

This Information Security Policy establishes the framework for protecting NexusPoint Systems' information assets, client data, and infrastructure. All employees, contractors, and third parties with access to NexusPoint systems must comply with this policy.

This policy applies to all information systems, including but not limited to: - Corporate networks and endpoints - Cloud infrastructure and services - Client environments under management - Remote access connections - Mobile devices and BYOD equipment

2. Information Security Objectives

NexusPoint Systems is committed to: - Maintaining confidentiality, integrity, and availability of all information assets - Protecting client data in accordance with contractual obligations and regulatory requirements - Ensuring business continuity and minimising security-related disruptions - Complying with applicable laws, regulations, and industry standards - Supporting our strategic pivot towards enhanced cybersecurity service delivery

3. Roles and Responsibilities

3.1 Executive Management

  • Alex Nguyen (Managing Director) maintains ultimate accountability for information security
  • Priya Sharma (Head of Service Delivery) ensures security controls are integrated into service delivery processes
  • Tanya Brooks (Finance & Operations Manager) oversees security budget allocation and vendor management

3.2 Cybersecurity Team

  • Marcus Webb (Cybersecurity Lead) implements and maintains security controls across all environments
  • Reports directly to Alex Nguyen on security posture and incidents
  • Coordinates with service delivery teams on client security requirements

3.3 All Staff

  • Jordan Reeves (People & Culture) ensures all new hires complete security awareness training within 5 business days
  • Sam Okoro (Service Desk Team Lead) acts as first point of escalation for security incidents
  • All employees must report suspected security incidents immediately

4. Acceptable Use Policy

4.1 General Principles

NexusPoint systems and resources are provided for legitimate business purposes. Personal use is permitted provided it: - Does not interfere with work responsibilities - Does not consume excessive resources - Complies with all other policy provisions

4.2 Prohibited Activities

The following activities are strictly forbidden: - Accessing, copying, or transmitting confidential client data without authorisation - Installing unauthorised software or circumventing security controls - Using company resources for illegal activities or personal financial gain - Sharing login credentials or allowing unauthorised access to systems - Connecting infected or compromised devices to company networks

4.3 Email and Communications

  • All business communications must use approved corporate email systems
  • Encryption is mandatory for emails containing sensitive client information
  • Personal cloud storage services (Dropbox, Google Drive, etc.) are prohibited for business data
  • Social media posts must not reference client information or ongoing projects

Note: The email encryption requirement is currently being rolled out - contact Marcus Webb for configuration assistance.

5. Access Control and Authentication

5.1 User Account Management

  • All system access requires prior approval from the employee's direct manager
  • Liam Foster (Senior Solutions Architect) maintains the master list of system access requirements by role
  • Accounts must be disabled within 24 hours of employment termination
  • Regular access reviews are conducted quarterly (next review due April 2024)

5.2 Password Requirements

  • Minimum 12 characters with complexity requirements
  • Multi-factor authentication (MFA) mandatory for all business systems
  • Password managers are required and provided by the company (1Password Enterprise)
  • Shared accounts are prohibited except for approved service accounts

5.3 Remote Access

  • VPN connection required for all remote access to corporate networks
  • Remote desktop access to client environments requires additional approval from Priya Sharma
  • All remote access sessions are logged and monitored

6. Data Classification and Handling

6.1 Classification Levels

Public: Information that can be freely shared (marketing materials, published documentation)

Internal: Information for internal use only (policies, procedures, internal communications)

Confidential: Sensitive business information requiring protection (financial data, strategic plans, employee records)

Client Confidential: Any information belonging to or about our clients (configurations, business data, credentials)

6.2 Handling Requirements

  • Client Confidential data must be encrypted at rest and in transit
  • Confidential and Client Confidential data requires approval for external sharing
  • All data must be stored in approved corporate systems or client-designated environments
  • Personal devices may not store business data above Internal classification

6.3 Data Retention

  • Client data retention follows contractual requirements (typically 7 years)
  • Employee records retained for 7 years post-termination
  • System logs retained for minimum 12 months
  • Secure disposal required for all storage media containing Confidential or Client Confidential data

7. Incident Management

7.1 Security Incident Definition

A security incident includes: - Unauthorised access to systems or data - Malware infections or suspected compromise - Data breaches or unauthorised disclosure - Denial of service attacks - Physical security breaches - Suspected insider threats

7.2 Incident Reporting

Immediate Response (within 30 minutes): - Contact Marcus Webb directly: +61 8 9xxx-xxxx or marcus.webb@nexuspoint.com.au - If Marcus unavailable, escalate to Priya Sharma or Alex Nguyen - Do not attempt to "fix" the issue - preserve evidence

Documentation Requirements: - Submit incident report within 2 hours using ServiceNow (incident template updated February 2024) - Include timeline, affected systems, potential data exposure, and initial containment actions - Client notification managed by Alex Nguyen in coordination with legal counsel

7.3 Incident Response Team

  • Incident Commander: Marcus Webb
  • Technical Lead: Liam Foster (backup: Sam Okoro)
  • Client Communications: Priya Sharma
  • External Communications: Alex Nguyen
  • Legal/Compliance: External counsel (Minter Ellison)

8. Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery

8.1 Backup Requirements

  • Critical client systems backed up hourly with 4-hour RPO
  • Corporate systems backed up daily with 24-hour RPO
  • All backups tested monthly (responsibility: Liam Foster)
  • Offsite backup storage maintained in separate geographic region

8.2 Recovery Objectives

  • RTO (Recovery Time Objective): 4 hours for critical client systems, 8 hours for corporate systems
  • RPO (Recovery Point Objective): 1 hour for critical client systems, 24 hours for corporate systems

Note: New backup solution implementation ongoing - expected completion May 2024.

9. Third Party and Vendor Management

9.1 Vendor Assessment

  • All technology vendors must complete security questionnaire before engagement
  • Annual security reviews required for critical vendors (AWS, Microsoft, security tools)
  • Vendor access to client environments requires specific client approval

9.2 Service Provider Requirements

  • SOC 2 Type II certification required for all critical service providers
  • Data processing agreements mandatory for any vendor handling client data
  • Regular penetration testing required for vendors with network access

10. Compliance and Legal Requirements

10.1 Regulatory Compliance

NexusPoint Systems complies with: - Privacy Act 1988 (Commonwealth) - Australian Consumer Law - Corporations Act 2001 - Industry-specific regulations as required by client contracts

10.2 International Standards

  • ISO 27001 certification pursuit planned for Q3 2024
  • NIST Cybersecurity Framework adopted as baseline security model
  • Essential Eight implementation in progress (currently at Maturity Level 2)

10.3 Client Contractual Requirements

  • Security controls implemented according to specific client SLAs
  • Regular compliance reporting to key clients (quarterly for mining sector clients)
  • Independent security assessments conducted annually

11. Security Awareness and Training

11.1 Training Requirements

  • Annual security awareness training mandatory for all staff
  • Phishing simulation testing conducted monthly
  • Role-specific training for technical staff handling client environments
  • New employee security orientation within first week

11.2 Communication

  • Monthly security updates distributed via company newsletter
  • Quarterly "lunch and learn" sessions on emerging threats
  • Annual Security Week event (next scheduled September 2024)

12. Monitoring and Audit

12.1 Security Monitoring

  • 24/7 monitoring of critical client environments
  • SIEM platform deployment in progress (expected completion June 2024)
  • User activity monitoring on all corporate systems
  • Network traffic analysis for anomaly detection

12.2 Internal Audits

  • Annual internal security audit conducted by external firm
  • Quarterly self-assessments using ISO 27001 framework
  • Monthly vulnerability assessments of all internet-facing systems
  • Penetration testing conducted annually (last completed November 2023)

13. Policy Violations and Disciplinary Action

13.1 Violation Reporting

Security policy violations must be reported to Jordan Reeves (People & Culture) and Marcus Webb within 24 hours of discovery.

13.2 Consequences

Violations may result in: - Verbal or written warnings - Mandatory additional training - Suspension of system access - Termination of employment - Legal action where appropriate

13.3 Investigation Process

  • All violations investigated by combined HR and security team
  • External legal counsel engaged for serious violations
  • Client notification required for violations affecting client data

14. Policy Review and Updates

This policy is reviewed annually and updated as needed to address: - Changes in business operations and client requirements - Emerging security threats and vulnerabilities - Regulatory and legal requirement changes - Lessons learned from security incidents

Next scheduled review: March 2025
Emergency updates: As required by Marcus Webb in consultation with Alex Nguyen

15. Related Documents

  • Employee Handbook (Section 8: IT Security)
  • Incident Response Procedures (IRP-001)
  • Business Continuity Plan (BCP-001)
  • Client Data Handling Procedures (DH-002)
  • Remote Work Security Guidelines (RW-001)

16. Contact Information

Security Team: - Marcus Webb, Cybersecurity Lead: marcus.webb@nexuspoint.com.au | +61 8 9xxx-xxxx - Emergency security hotline: +61 8 9xxx-9999 (24/7)

Management: - Alex Nguyen, Managing Director: alex.nguyen@nexuspoint.com.au - Priya Sharma, Head of Service Delivery: priya.sharma@nexuspoint.com.au

HR Support: - Jordan Reeves, People & Culture: jordan.reeves@nexuspoint.com.au

This document contains confidential and proprietary information of NexusPoint Systems Pty Ltd. Distribution is restricted to authorised personnel only.

Document Control:
